lessons.title: Zoo Animals
lessons.topic: A virtual trip to the zoo
lessons.context: A virtual trip to the zoo.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where do zoo animals live?
questions.correct_answer: In special homes at the zoo
options: In special homes at the zoo
questions.hint: Think about where you go to see lions and elephants!

questions.question_text: What can you see at the zoo?
questions.hint: The zoo has many kinds of animals like giraffes and monkeys!

questions.question_text: How do you visit a zoo?
questions.correct_answer: Walk around and look at the animals
options: Walk around and look at the animals
questions.hint: You use your eyes to see all the amazing animals!

items.definition: An animal with a long neck.
A large, water-loving animal.
An animal with black and white stripes.
An animal with no legs.
A tall animal with a long neck
A big animal that loves water
An animal with black and white stripes
A long animal with no legs

steps.title: My Virtual Zoo Trip
steps.text: Today I visit the zoo on my computer! I see a tall giraffe. It eats leaves from trees. The hippo lives in water. It has got a big mouth! The zebra has black and white stripes. Look, there is a long snake in the grass.
steps.scene_description: child looking at computer screen showing zoo animals, smiling and pointing at screen

questions.question: Where does the hippo live?
questions.locate_text: The hippo lives in water. It has got a big mouth!

questions.question: Has the zebra got stripes?
questions.locate_text: The zebra has black and white stripes.

steps.rule_pattern: It ___ (verb). / Has it got ___? / It has got ___ (description).
steps.explanation: We use 'it' to talk about one animal and tell what the animal does or what it has got.
examples: Has it got a long neck? Yes, it has!
The zebra has got black and white stripes.

items.correctSentence: It has got a big mouth

items.sentence: Has the hippo got big mouth?

items.explanation: This is correct! Use 'eats' with giraffe.

items.sentence: Has it got a long neck?
items.corrected: Has it got a long neck?
items.explanation: This is correct! Good question form.

items.sentence: The snake has got no legs.
items.corrected: The snake has got no legs.

steps.audio_text: Hello, children! Today we're going on a virtual zoo trip. Look at the screen! Can you see the tall giraffe? It eats leaves from the trees. Now look at the hippo. It lives in the water and has got a very big mouth! Oh, there's a zebra! It has got black and white stripes. And look in the grass... there's a long snake!

steps.prompt: Let's talk about zoo animals! Listen and repeat, then answer the questions.

prompts.prompt: Say: The hippo lives in water.
Now you! What has the zebra got?

questions.question_text: Which animal has got a long neck?
The hippo ___ in water.
True or False: The zebra has got black and white stripes.
What does the giraffe eat?
Has the snake got legs?

lessons.title: Small Animals
lessons.topic: Looking for tiny animals in a garden
lessons.context: Looking for tiny animals in a garden.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where can we find tiny animals in a garden?
questions.hint: Think about dark, quiet places where small animals like to hide!

questions.question_text: Which tiny animal can we see in a garden?
questions.hint: This tiny animal is red with black spots and can fly!

questions.question_text: What should we do when we find a tiny animal?
options: Pick it up and put it in our pocket
questions.hint: Tiny animals are very small and delicate. We need to be kind and quiet!

items.definition: A small bug with eight legs.
A small animal that hops.
A small animal with a long tail.
items.example: A lizard is on the wall.

items.definition: The part that sticks out at the back of an animal.

items.hint: This tiny animal has eight legs
This green animal jumps and says 'ribbit'
This animal has four legs and a long tail
The long part at the back of an animal's body

items.sentence: Look! A ___ is making a web.
The green ___ jumps into the pond.
The ___ is sitting on the rock in the sun.
The lizard has a very long ___.

steps.title: In the Garden
steps.text: Emma goes to the garden. She looks under a leaf. A spider is there! It has eight legs. Emma looks near the pond. A green frog jumps in the water. Then she sees a lizard on a rock. It has a long tail.
steps.scene_description: young girl exploring garden looking at plants and small animals

questions.question: Where does Emma find the spider?
What colour is the frog?
questions.locate_text: A green frog jumps in the water.

questions.question: The lizard has got a long tail. Yes or No?

steps.rule_pattern: It has got ___ / Has it got ___?
steps.explanation: We use 'has got' to talk about what animals have on their bodies.
examples: The spider has got eight legs.
The lizard has got a long tail.
Has the frog got wings? No, it hasn't.

items.correctSentence: The spider has got eight legs
The lizard has got four legs

items.sentence: The spider have got eight legs.
items.explanation: Use 'has' with it/he/she or single things
Use plural 'wings' because frogs don't have wings anyway, but if they did, there would be two
Add 's' to verbs with it/he/she or single animals

items.sentence: Him has got a long tail.
The spider has got eight legs.
items.corrected: The spider has got eight legs.
items.explanation: This is correct! Use 'has got' with single animals.
This is correct! Use 'Has' to ask questions.
This is correct! Use 'eats' with single animals.

items.question: The spider ___ got eight legs.
items.explanation: Use 'has got' with it/he/she or single animals

items.question: The lizard ___ small bugs.
items.explanation: Add 's' to make 'eats' with single animals

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Today we are looking for tiny animals in the garden. Look! I can see a spider under this leaf. It has got eight legs. Now let's look by the pond. Can you see the green frog? It's jumping! And there's a lizard on that rock. It has got a very long tail.

questions.question: How many legs has the spider got?

steps.prompt: Now it's your turn to speak! Say these sentences about tiny animals.
steps.expected_text: The spider has got eight legs

prompts.prompt: Look at the spider. Say: The spider has got eight legs.
prompts.expected_text: The spider has got eight legs

prompts.prompt: Look at the frog. Say: The frog lives in the garden.
prompts.expected_text: The frog lives in the garden

prompts.prompt: Look at the lizard. Ask: Has it got a long tail?
prompts.expected_text: Has it got a long tail

questions.question_text: Which animal has got eight legs?
The lizard ___ got a long tail.
Where do tiny animals live in the garden?
True or False: A frog eats small flies.
___ it got a tail?

lessons.title: Descriptions
lessons.topic: Guessing an animal based on physical clues
lessons.context: Guessing an animal based on physical clues.

steps.instruction: Let's think about animals! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Which animal has a long neck?
questions.hint: This animal is very tall and eats leaves from trees!

questions.question_text: Which animal has big ears?
questions.hint: This animal is big and grey with a long nose called a trunk!

questions.question_text: Which animal can jump very high?
questions.hint: This animal has strong back legs and carries babies in a pouch!

items.definition: A neck that is tall.
Very large ears on an animal or person.
Something that frightens you.
Makes you laugh.

steps.title: Let's Guess the Animal!
steps.text: Emma plays a guessing game with her friends. She says, 'This animal has a long neck. It eats leaves from tall trees. It lives in Africa. It has big ears too!' Tom says, 'Is it scary?' Emma laughs, 'No, it's funny when it runs!'
steps.scene_description: children playing guessing game in classroom, pointing at animal pictures on wall

questions.question: What animal are the children talking about?
questions.locate_text: She says, 'This animal has a long neck. It eats leaves from tall trees.

questions.question: Does the animal eat leaves from tall trees?
questions.locate_text: It eats leaves from tall trees. It lives in Africa.

questions.question: Is the animal scary?
questions.locate_text: She says, 'This animal has a long neck. It eats leaves from tall trees.

steps.explanation: We use 'has' or 'has got' to describe what animals have on their bodies.

items.correctSentence: Has it got a long neck

items.sentence: The lion has two big tooth.
It has got a long neck.
items.corrected: It has got a long neck.

items.explanation: Add 's' to the verb with he/she/it
Correct! This is the right question form
Correct! 'Is' is right with singular nouns

items.question: ___ it live in Africa?
items.explanation: Use 'Does' for Present Simple questions with 'it'

items.question: Has the lion got ___ scary teeth?
items.explanation: Use 'any' in questions with plural nouns

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Let's play a guessing game. I'm thinking of an animal. It has a very long neck and big ears. It lives in Africa and eats leaves from tall trees. Is it scary? No! It's funny when it runs. What animal is it?

questions.question: What does the animal have?
questions.answer: a long neck and big ears
options: a long tail and big eyes
a long neck and big ears
a short neck and small ears

steps.prompt: Now it's your turn! Describe an animal using the words we learned.

prompts.prompt: Look at the giraffe. Say: It has a long neck.
Look at the elephant. Say: It has got big ears.
Think of a funny animal. Say: The monkey is funny.

questions.question_text: Which animal has a long neck?
It ___ big ears.
The giraffe ___ leaves from tall trees.
True or False: A lion is scary.
Which sentence is correct?

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.topic: Sorting animals by size and habitat
lessons.context: Sorting animals by size and habitat.

steps.instruction: Let's think about animals! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Which animal is the biggest?
questions.hint: This animal has a long trunk and is very, very big!

questions.question_text: Where does a fish live?
questions.hint: Fish swim! Where can they swim?

questions.question_text: Which animal is the smallest?
questions.hint: This animal is so tiny you can step on it by accident!

items.definition: A tall animal with a very long neck
A big, fat animal that lives in water
An animal with black and white stripes
A long animal with no legs
A small animal with eight legs
A green animal that jumps and says ribbit
The long part at the back of an animal
Something that makes you afraid
A tall animal with a very long neck
A small animal with four legs and a long tail
An animal with black and white stripes
A body part that is very tall
Large body parts used for hearing
Something that makes you laugh
The long part at the back of an animal
A green animal that jumps and says ribbit

items.question: Which animal has a very long neck?
What is at the back of many animals?
Which animal has black and white stripes?
Which word means something makes you laugh?
Which animal is big and lives in water?
Which animal jumps and says ribbit?

steps.title: Animals at the Zoo
steps.text: Emma goes to the zoo with her class. She sees a tall giraffe with a long neck. It eats leaves from the trees. Then she sees a hippo in the water. It has got big ears and a big mouth. The snake is small and scary. It lives in the grass.
steps.scene_description: children looking at animals at the zoo, giraffe and hippo visible

questions.question: Where does the hippo live?
questions.locate_text: Then she sees a hippo in the water. It has got big ears and a big mouth.

questions.question: Has the giraffe got a long neck?
questions.locate_text: She sees a tall giraffe with a long neck. It eats leaves from the trees.

questions.question: What does the giraffe eat?
questions.locate_text: It eats leaves from the trees.

steps.rule_pattern: It ___ (verb). / Has it got ___? / It has got ___ (body part).
steps.explanation: We use 'it' to talk about one animal, and we can describe what it does, where it lives, and what body parts it has.
examples: Has it got a long neck? Yes, it has!
It eats leaves from the trees.
The giraffe has got big ears.

items.sentence: The giraffe have got a tail.
Has the snake got two tail?
The snake have a long tail.
items.corrected: The snake has a long tail.

items.question: ___ the zebra got stripes?
items.explanation: Use 'Has' to ask about body parts

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Today we learned about animals. The giraffe is very tall. It has got a long neck and it eats leaves. The hippo is big and it lives in the water. The snake is small and scary. It lives in the grass. Now, can you tell me which animals are big and which are small?

questions.question: Which animal has got a long neck?

steps.prompt: Let's talk about animals! Listen and repeat, then answer the questions.

prompts.prompt: Say: It lives in the water.
Say: The giraffe has got a long neck.
prompts.expected_text: The giraffe has got a long neck

prompts.prompt: Now you try! Where does the hippo live?
Has the giraffe got big ears?
Tell me about one animal. What does it look like?
prompts.expected_text: The snake is long and scary

questions.question_text: Which animal has got a long neck?
The hippo ___ in the water.
___ the snake got legs?
True or False: The giraffe eats leaves from the trees.
Where does a fish live?
